title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_107 × Z_25) prime?
generalization_signal: Skeptical verification supports the post-97 complementary-support direction on the four-class Γ(Z_p × Z_25) line: for p = 107, the fixed A0 reservoir and the high barrier {1,293,307,311} still work once C is defined by support avoidance rather than a rigid prime-only block. The structural support-template story scales, but the exact C cutoff and the longevity of this barrier set remain finite-instance arithmetic choices.
strongest_honest_claim: An explicit prime-labeling witness for Γ(Z_107 × Z_25) survives skeptical verification. The campaign-relevant gain is not a Lean-closed exact theorem, but verified feeder evidence that the active F25 line continues beyond the preserved p <= 97 window under a complementary-support C-block construction with the existing high barrier.
candidate_theorem_slice: A post-97 high-barrier complementary-support slice for Γ(Z_p × Z_25): fix A on the A0 reservoir, choose B as pairwise-coprime barrier labels above half the active interval, choose C from labels whose prime support avoids A ∪ B, and let D absorb the complement.
next_action: Run generalize on the post-97 complementary-support pattern and test the next feeder p = 109 before attempting any instance-level Lean packaging.
